aids.title: MCM-PHM = Multivariate Statistics Core
aids.content: **M**ean vector, **C**ovariance matrix, **M**ahalanobis distance, **P**rincipal components, **H**otelling's T², **M**ANOVA. Remember: 'My Cow Mooed Past Harry's Manor' for the six core concepts.
aids.forRemembering: All six key topics in multivariate statistics

aids.title: The Rubber Sheet Analogy
aids.content: Imagine data points plotted on a rubber sheet that is stretched along the direction of high variance. Euclidean distance measures with a rigid ruler (ignoring the stretch), so points along the stretched direction seem far. Mahalanobis distance uses a flexible ruler that accounts for the stretch, giving a true sense of how unusual a point is relative to the data's natural shape.
aids.forRemembering: Why Mahalanobis distance is better than Euclidean

aids.title: Covariance Matrix vs. Correlation Matrix
aids.content: Covariance matrix = raw relationships (scale matters). Correlation matrix = standardized relationships (scale removed). To convert: divide each covariance by the product of the two standard deviations. Diagonal of covariance = variances; diagonal of correlation = always 1.
aids.forRemembering: Distinguishing the two matrices in exams
